Array.description: The fundamental multidimensional container type parameterized by element type T and dimensionality N. Arrays in Julia are column-major, mutable, and support broadcasting, comprehensions, and high-performance linear algebra operations through BLAS integration.

push!.description: Appends one or more items to the end of the collection, mutating it in place and returning it.

pop!.description: Removes and returns the last element from the collection.

length.description: Returns the total number of elements in the collection.

size.description: Returns a tuple of dimensions of array A, or the size along a specific dimension if dim is given.

sort!.signature: sort!(v; alg=defalg, lt=isless, by=identity, rev=false) -> v
sort!.description: Sorts the vector v in place using the specified algorithm and comparison functions.

filter.description: Returns a new array containing only the elements of a for which function f returns true.

map.description: Transforms each element by applying function f, returning a new collection of results.

reshape.description: Returns an array with the same data as A but with different dimensions specified by dims.

Dict.description: A hash-table-based dictionary mapping keys of type K to values of type V. Supports iteration over key-value pairs, efficient lookup, insertion, and deletion. Dict is the standard mutable associative container in Julia's Base library.

get.description: Returns the value for key if it exists in the collection, otherwise returns the default value.

haskey.description: Determines whether the collection has a mapping for the given key, returning true or false.

keys.description: Returns an iterator over all keys in the dictionary.

values.description: Returns an iterator over all values in the dictionary.

delete!.description: Deletes the mapping for the given key from the collection and returns the modified collection.

merge.description: Constructs a merged dictionary from the given dictionaries, with later values overwriting earlier ones for duplicate keys.

String.description: An immutable UTF-8 encoded string type. Strings in Julia support Unicode natively, are indexed by byte position (not character index), and provide extensive pattern matching, interpolation with $ syntax, and conversion utilities.

occursin.description: Determines whether the first argument is a substring of the second, supporting both string and regex patterns.

replace.description: Returns a new string with occurrences of pat replaced by r, optionally limiting the number of replacements.

split.description: Splits the string at each occurrence of the delimiter and returns a vector of substrings.

join.description: Concatenates an iterable collection of strings with the given delimiter between elements.

strip.description: Removes leading and trailing whitespace or specified characters from the string.

Tuple.description: An immutable, fixed-size, ordered collection of elements, each potentially having a different type. Tuples are used extensively for multiple return values, function argument packing, and as dictionary keys due to their immutability and efficient memory layout.

length.description: Returns the number of elements in the tuple.

first.description: Returns the first element of the tuple.

last.description: Returns the last element of the tuple.

Set.description: An unordered collection of unique elements of type T, backed by a Dict. Sets support standard mathematical operations including union, intersection, difference, and symmetric difference, as well as membership testing with the in operator.

union.description: Constructs the union of sets, containing all elements that appear in any of the arguments.

intersect.description: Constructs the intersection of sets, containing only elements common to all arguments.

setdiff.description: Constructs the set of elements in s but not in any of the other iterables.

issubset.description: Determines whether every element of a is also in b.

push!.description: Adds element x to the set, mutating it in place.

println.description: Prints the textual representation of the arguments to the output stream (defaulting to stdout), followed by a newline character. Each argument is converted to a string via the show function. Multiple arguments are printed consecutively without separators.

typeof.description: Returns the concrete type of the value x. This is the leaf type in Julia's type hierarchy and is used for method dispatch, type assertions, and runtime introspection. Combined with supertypes, it reveals the full type ancestry.

struct.signature: struct TypeName fields... end | mutable struct TypeName fields... end
struct.description: Declares a composite type with named fields. Immutable structs (the default) are stack-allocated and more efficiently optimized by the compiler. Mutable structs are heap-allocated and allow field reassignment. Both support constructors, type parameters, and abstract supertype declarations.

function.description: Defines a named function with typed or untyped arguments, optional return type annotation, and a body block. Julia functions support multiple dispatch — defining multiple methods with different argument type signatures that are selected at call time based on the types of all arguments.

Channel.description: A typed concurrent communication primitive used for passing messages between asynchronous tasks. Channels can be buffered (with a specified size) or unbuffered, and support put!/take! operations for producer-consumer patterns in Julia's cooperative multitasking model.

put!.description: Appends a value to the channel, blocking if the channel buffer is full until space is available.

take!.description: Removes and returns a value from the channel, blocking if empty until a value is available.

close.description: Closes the channel so that no more values can be put into it; existing values can still be taken.

isopen.description: Determines whether the channel is still open for putting new values.
